title: tragia benthamii
intro: Tragia benthamii, also known as the poison nut, is a plant species that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Tragia benthamii include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations of Tragia benthamii are typically made from its leaves, stems, or roots, and can be administered as teas, infusions, or extracts to harness its medicinal properties.
